name: cloudflare-blade-mcp
title: Cloudflare Edge Platform
description: Manage your Cloudflare stack — DNS, Workers, Pages, R2, KV, D1, Tunnels, and cache purge. 53 tools with token-efficient output and safety gates on all writes.
tagline: Full-stack Cloudflare management from DNS to deploy
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: DNS — zones, records, bulk import/export across all your domains
Workers — scripts, deployments, versions, secrets, cron schedules
Pages — projects, builds, rollback, domains, cache purge
R2 — bucket lifecycle, location hints, storage classes
KV — namespaces, key-value CRUD, bulk operations
D1 — databases, tables, queries, schema inspection, export

readme:
### What it does

Cloudflare Edge Platform gives your AI agent full operational control over your Cloudflare infrastructure — DNS records, Workers, Pages, R2, KV, D1, Tunnels, and cache — through 53 purpose-built tools.

Your agent can check DNS propagation, roll back a broken Pages deploy, rotate a Workers secret, query a D1 database, and purge cache in a single conversation. No context switching between dashboards.

### Token efficiency

Every Cloudflare API response is large and noisy. Raw responses waste context window on pagination metadata, empty fields, and internal IDs your agent will never use.

Each tool includes a **concise formatter** that extracts only the fields that matter. A typical `list_zones` call returns ~200 tokens instead of ~800. Across a session with dozens of API calls, this saves thousands of tokens and keeps your agent focused.

### Safety model

All 53 tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — list zones, get records, inspect deployments
- **Writes** require `confirm: true` — create records, put secrets, purge cache
- **Destructive operations** are double-gated — delete records, remove secrets, drop buckets

This means your agent can explore your infrastructure freely but must be explicit about changes. Accidental deletions are structurally prevented.

### Domain coverage

| Domain | Tools | What you can do |
|--------|-------|-----------------|
| DNS | 10 | Zones, records, bulk create/update, BIND export |
| Workers | 10 | Scripts, deployments, versions, secrets, cron triggers |
| Pages | 7 | Projects, builds, rollback, custom domains, build cache |
| R2 | 4 | Buckets with location hints, jurisdiction, storage class |
| KV | 7 | Namespaces, key CRUD, bulk put/delete |
| D1 | 7 | Databases, tables, SQL query/execute, schema, export |
| Tunnels | 7 | Create, configure, connections, ingress routing |
| Cache | 1 | Purge by URL, tag, host, prefix, or everything |

env.name: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN
env.description: Cloudflare API token with Zone DNS Edit, Workers Scripts/KV/D1/Tunnel/R2 Edit, Pages Read, Cache Purge permissions

env.name: CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
env.description: Cloudflare Account ID — required for KV, D1, Tunnel, Workers, Pages, and R2 tools

env.name: MCP_API_TOKEN
env.description: Bearer token for HTTP transport authentication (optional, only needed for HTTP mode)

setup.blurb: Manage DNS, Workers, Pages, R2, KV, D1, Tunnels, and Cache. 53 tools with token-efficient output.
setup.help_text: Create a custom API token at dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens. Grant Zone > DNS > Edit, Account > Workers Scripts/KV/D1/Tunnel/R2 > Edit, and Zone > Cache Purge > Purge permissions. Tokens can be scoped to specific zones if you only need DNS.

fields.label: API Token
fields.help: Custom token with Zone DNS Edit and Account-level KV/D1/Tunnel Edit permissions

fields.label: Account ID
fields.help: Found on any zone's overview page in the Cloudflare dashboard, or via the API

scenarios.label: Deploy and verify a Worker update
scenarios.body: List Worker versions, create a new deployment with traffic split, check cron schedules are intact, then purge cache for the affected routes.

scenarios.label: Investigate a broken Pages deploy
scenarios.body: List recent Pages deployments, compare production vs preview builds, check custom domain DNS records, and rollback to the last successful deploy if needed.

scenarios.label: Audit infrastructure for a domain migration
scenarios.body: Export DNS records for the old zone, list D1 databases and KV namespaces tied to the domain's Workers, and verify tunnel configurations before cutting over.
